markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Expose local graph neighborhoods with filters, edge types, depth, stale markers, and source citations. This MT belongs to LoomObsidianNavigation. Deliver the Obsidian replacement surface as a Handshake-native Loom projection.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Obsidian-like behavior must be implemented as Loom/project knowledge projections over PostgreSQL, not a markdown-vault clone.
Backlinks, graph, tags, folders, pins, color labels, and project links must have backend navigation APIs and visual-debug evidence.
scope.expected_behavior: LocalGraphApi leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: LocalGraphApi is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence.summary: Local graph neighborhood API (undirected BFS, edge-type filters, depth, stale markers, ProjectKnowledgeIndex citations) over loom_edges + loom_blocks + the MT-177 bridge. Read projection; no parallel store.
files_changed: src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs - local_graph (UNDIRECTED recursive CTE, both edge directions, cycle-guarded, node_limit -> truncated) + shared assemble_loom_graph (induced subgraph, per-node degree, stale=not-bridged, ai_suggested edge stale)

evidence.result: ok. 5 passed; 0 failed (real PostgreSQL) [shared MT-179+180 suite]
tests: local_graph_is_undirected_with_stale_markers_and_citations - reaches incoming A + outgoing C from B; bridged node not-stale w/ entity_id citation; un-bridged node stale w/o citation; ai_suggested edge stale; mention-only filter drops tag neighbor

evidence: Status flip note (2026-06-12T06:25:27.785Z): Loom engine group backend-complete per delegate report - real-PG proven (isolated schemas, full migration chain), per-MT adversarial review recorded in this file. SQLite removed/unreachable (orphans git-rm'd, sqlx has no sqlite feature, source regression guard added). Markdown-vault-never-authority proven via projection-delete byte-identity negative test. Stable /workspaces/:ws/loom/* HTTP contracts documented for the Notes UI. Implementation commit(s): cd944110.

findings: INT4/INT8 DECODE (fixed): recursive depth literal is INT4; MIN(depth) cast ::BIGINT to match the i64 decode. Proven by now-green depth tests.
DOS (mitigated): node_limit.clamp(1,5000) + max_depth clamp(1,8) + path cycle guard.
EDGE-TYPE INJECTION (clear): LoomEdgeType enum parse; node ids bound as TEXT[].
DETERMINISM (clear): explicit ORDER BY in CTE + node assembly.
CITATION/STALE (handled+tested): stale = no bridge row; citation = bridge entity_id.
fixes: Cast MIN(depth)::BIGINT in the local-graph CTE.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-179 requires a Handshake-native local graph neighborhood API with edge-type filters, depth, stale markers, and ProjectKnowledgeIndex source citations as a PostgreSQL-backed Loom projection. The code delivers exactly this (undirected cycle-guarded recursive-CTE BFS + induced-subgraph assembly with degree/stale/citation), wired to GET /loom/graph/local, and proven against real PostgreSQL with full migrations.
findings: storage/postgres.rs:5312-5395 local_graph: fail-closed start check (get_loom_block), depth.max(1), node_limit.clamp(1,5000), UNDIRECTED recursive CTE walking both source->target and target->source with ARRAY path cycle guard and edge-type filter ($4 TEXT[]), MIN(depth)::BIGINT, fetch cap+1 to detect truncation. Real, non-placeholder.

storage/postgres.rs:71-195 assemble_loom_graph: fetches blocks for the node set, loom_block_knowledge_bridge entity_id citations, induced edges (both endpoints in node set) within edge-type filter, per-node degree, stale=not-bridged for nodes, stale=AiSuggested for edges, deterministic ORDER BY. Matches MT requirement of stale markers + source citations.
storage/loom.rs:485-526 LoomGraphNode/Edge/Graph carry depth, degree, stale, entity_id citation, truncated, suppressed_hub_ids; serde skips on absent. Matches the projection contract.

api/loom.rs:1099-1124 local_loom_graph handler validates workspace + start_block_id (HSK-400-LOOM-START-BLOCK-REQUIRED), clamps depth, parses edge_types, node_limit min 5000, calls storage.local_graph; route registered at api/loom.rs:140 /workspaces/:workspace_id/loom/graph/local.

tests/loom_graph_tests.rs:93-180 three MT-179 tests run against REAL PostgreSQL via knowledge_pg_support.rs (isolated CREATE SCHEMA, PostgresDatabase::connect + run_migrations full chain, search_path pinned): undirected reachability of incoming A + outgoing C from B, depth-0 start, bridged=non-stale with entity_id citation, un-bridged=stale w/o citation, ai_suggested edge stale, mention-only filter drops tag neighbor; depth-1 induced edges=2; fail-closed NotFound on missing start.
No deferred-live escapes: grep found only descriptive comments ('not yet an indexed entity'); no todo!/unimplemented!/Unavailable on live paths.
SQLite unreachable: Cargo.toml sqlx has only the postgres feature (no sqlite); storage/loom.rs:862-864 is an explicit PostgreSQL-only regression-guard comment. No mock-only or SQLite substitution.

Aside (non-blocking, Integration-Validator whole-WP concern): current spec [LM-GRAPH-001] (11-shared-dev-platform...md:601) still mentions SQLite dual-store; the packet's DEC-008 PostgreSQL-only direction supersedes this. Spec-pointer/legacy text drift is not a per-MT code defect.
evidence_checked: storage/loom.rs LoomGraph types + SQLite guard comment

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
